Container.Name: OpenClaw
Container.Overview: OpenClaw is a self-hosted AI assistant gateway that connects Claude, GPT, Gemini, Groq, and any OpenAI/Anthropic-compatible LLM router to messaging platforms like Discord, Telegram, WhatsApp, Slack, and more. Features file access, shell commands, browser control, cron jobs, and a web-based Control UI. === REQUIRED ON FIRST INSTALL === 1. Gateway Token (any secret -- generate with: openssl rand -hex 24) 2. Allowed Origins -- your Unraid host URL with the Control UI port, e.g. http://192.168.1.41:18789. Comma-separated for multiple. WITHOUT THIS THE GATEWAY REFUSES TO START since OpenClaw 2026.2. 3. ONE LLM source -- either a built-in provider key (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Groq, OpenRouter, xAI, Z.AI) OR the Custom LLM trio (Base URL + API Key + API Type) for your own router (LiteLLM, vLLM, Ollama, etc.) === POST-INSTALL STEPS === 1. Open Control UI: http://YOUR-UNRAID-IP:18789/?token=YOUR-GATEWAY-TOKEN (the ?token= part is mandatory). 2. If you used a non-Anthropic provider OR the Custom LLM endpoint, change the default model: Config tab -> Agents -> Raw JSON -> set agents.defaults.model.primary to match your provider (e.g. google/gemini-2.0-flash, openai/gpt-4o, custom/your-model-id). Save and restart the container. 3. (Optional) Connect a messaging channel: Config tab -> Channels. Or set Discord/Telegram bot tokens in this template, then approve pairing in the Agents tab on first message. 4. (Optional) Install Homebrew if a skill needs go/npm tools -- open the container console and run: NONINTERACTIVE=1 /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)" Review brew.sh before running. The template already configures PATH. === SMB / NFS VISIBILITY === Container runs as root. Bootstrap auto-aligns ownership with the mount root every 5s, but you must set ownership on the host ONCE so it has a target. Replace YOUR_UID:YOUR_GID with your values (find with: id $USER; Unraid default = 99:100): chown -R YOUR_UID:YOUR_GID /mnt/user/appdata/openclaw chmod -R g+rwX,o+rX /mnt/user/appdata/openclaw find /mnt/user/appdata/openclaw -type d -exec chmod g+s {} + openclaw.json stays mode 0600 by design (contains gateway token + api keys); only owner reads it, not group. === DO NOT USE === CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN / claude setup-token: Anthropic prohibits using Claude Code subscription tokens with third-party tooling. Use a real API key from console.anthropic.com instead. === TROUBLESHOOTING === "origin not allowed" -> Allowed Origins value must match the URL in your browser EXACTLY (scheme + host + port). Edit, Apply, restart. "No API key found for provider 'anthropic'" -> You used a non-Anthropic key without changing the default model. See post-install step 2. Container goes to STOP after gateway restart -> should not happen with default --restart=unless-stopped. If your Restart Policy got overridden to 'no', set it back to Unless Stopped. Files invisible via SMB -> run the SMB / NFS VISIBILITY commands above. Full guide: https://github.com/thebtf/openclaw-unraid OpenClaw project: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw OpenClaw docs: https://docs.openclaw.ai
Container.Description: OpenClaw is a self-hosted AI assistant gateway. Access the Control UI at http://YOUR-UNRAID-IP:18789/?token=YOUR-GATEWAY-TOKEN. Requires Gateway Token, Allowed Origins, and one LLM API key or custom LLM endpoint.

Config.Name: Control UI Port
Config.Description: Port for Control UI and Gateway API. Access at http://IP:18789/?token=YOUR_TOKEN

Config.Name: Config Path
Config.Description: Path to store OpenClaw configuration files, sessions, and credentials

Config.Name: Workspace Path
Config.Description: Path for agent workspace (files, memory, AGENTS.md, projects)

Config.Name: Projects Path
Config.Description: Optional additional path for coding projects

Config.Name: Homebrew Path
Config.Description: Persistent storage for Homebrew packages. If you install Homebrew manually via console, this mount ensures it persists across restarts.

Config.Name: Local Tools Path
Config.Description: Persistent storage for ~/.local (pip --user installs, manually-built CLIs in /root/.local/bin, libs in /root/.local/lib). Survives container restarts. PATH already includes /root/.local/bin.

Config.Name: Logs Path
Config.Description: OpenClaw gateway log files. Mounted to /tmp/openclaw in the container -- the runtime always writes there regardless of logging.file config (see openclaw issue #61295). Mounting it out keeps logs off the container overlay fs. Built-in rotation: ~25 MB per file (configurable via OPENCLAW_LOG_MAX_FILE_BYTES), 5 numbered archives kept (hardcoded in openclaw).

Config.Name: Gateway Token
Config.Description: Secret token for authentication. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 24 (or use any secret value). Required to access the Control UI.

Config.Name: Allowed Origins
Config.Description: REQUIRED. Browser origins allowed to connect to the Control UI. Comma-separated list of full URLs (scheme + host + port). Example: http://192.168.1.41:18789 or http://192.168.1.41:18789,http://openclaw.local:18789. Without this the gateway refuses to start (OpenClaw 2026.2+ enforces this on non-loopback hosts). Use your Unraid IP and the Control UI Port above.

Config.Name: Disable Device Auth
Config.Description: Disable browser device-identity check. Default 'true' for LAN/homelab use over plain HTTP -- without this you get 'control ui requires device identity (use HTTPS or localhost secure context)' because Web Crypto API needs a secure context (HTTPS or http://localhost). Token auth is still required regardless. Set to 'false' if you access the Control UI via a real HTTPS reverse proxy and want full device-identity protection.

Config.Name: Log Max File Bytes
Config.Description: (Optional) Max bytes per log file before rotation. Default 26214400 (25 MB). OpenClaw rotates to openclaw-YYYY-MM-DD.1.log etc when reached. Total disk = ~6 * this value (5 archives + active).

Config.Name: Skip Permission Fix
Config.Description: (Optional) Set to 1 to disable the generic permission fix (chown --reference, umask 0002, setgid on dirs, runtime owner-sync). Disable only if you manage permissions externally.

Config.Name: Perm Fix Interval (seconds)
Config.Description: (Optional) Interval for the runtime owner-sync loop. OpenClaw rotates config and writes session files continuously; this loop re-aligns owner with the mount root every N seconds so SMB/NFS clients see consistent ownership. Default 5. Increase to 30+ on slow disks; set to 0 (with Skip Permission Fix=1) to disable.

Config.Name: Custom LLM Base URL
Config.Description: (Optional) Base URL for a custom LLM endpoint. OpenAI-compatible: http://litellm:4000/v1 or http://ollama:11434/v1. Anthropic-compatible: https://my-anthropic-router.example.com. Leave empty if using built-in providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.) below.

Config.Name: Custom LLM API Key
Config.Description: (Optional) API key for the custom LLM endpoint above. Use 'ollama' for local Ollama, your router token otherwise. Required if Custom LLM Base URL is set.

Config.Name: Custom LLM API Type
Config.Description: (Optional) Protocol adapter for the custom endpoint. THIS IS NOT THE MODEL NAME -- put the model name in 'Custom LLM Model ID' below. Allowed values: openai-completions (LiteLLM, vLLM, Ollama, OpenRouter), openai-responses (new OpenAI Responses API), openai-codex-responses, anthropic-messages, google-generative-ai, github-copilot, bedrock-converse-stream, ollama, azure-openai-responses.

Config.Name: Custom LLM Model ID
Config.Description: (Optional) Model identifier(s) exposed by your custom endpoint. Required if Custom LLM Base URL is set. Examples: gpt-5.5, llama-3.1-70b, claude-3-opus. Comma-separated for multiple: gpt-5.5,claude-3-opus. To use this model, set agents.defaults.model.primary to 'custom/MODEL_ID' in Control UI Raw JSON after install.

Config.Name: Custom LLM Context Window
Config.Description: (Optional) Total context window in tokens for the custom model(s). Look up your actual model's value -- e.g. gpt-4o = 128000, gpt-4-turbo = 128000, claude-3-opus = 200000, gpt-5 = 400000. Default 128000. If you have multiple models with different windows, edit Raw JSON post-install for per-model values.

Config.Name: Custom LLM Max Tokens
Config.Description: (Optional) Max output tokens per response for the custom model(s). E.g. gpt-4o = 16384, claude-3-opus = 4096, gpt-5 = 32000. Default 32000. Adjust if your provider rejects with 'max_tokens too high'.

Config.Name: Anthropic API Key
Config.Description: API key for Claude models. Get from console.anthropic.com (separate from Claude Pro/Max subscription).

Config.Name: OpenAI API Key
Config.Description: API key for GPT models. Get from platform.openai.com

Config.Name: OpenRouter API Key
Config.Description: API key for OpenRouter - access 100+ models. Get from openrouter.ai

Config.Name: Gemini API Key
Config.Description: API key for Google Gemini models. Get from aistudio.google.com

Config.Name: Groq API Key
Config.Description: API key for Groq (fast Llama/Mixtral inference). Get from console.groq.com

Config.Name: xAI API Key
Config.Description: API key for xAI Grok models. Get from console.x.ai

Config.Name: Z.AI API Key
Config.Description: API key for Z.AI/Zhipu GLM models. Get from z.ai/model-api

Config.Name: GitHub Copilot Token
Config.Description: (Advanced) GitHub Copilot OAuth token. Requires running 'openclaw models auth login-github-copilot' on another machine first.

Config.Name: Discord Bot Token
Config.Description: (Optional) Discord bot token for Discord integration. Can also configure via Control UI after install.

Config.Name: Telegram Bot Token
Config.Description: (Optional) Telegram bot token from @BotFather. Can also configure via Control UI after install.

Config.Name: Gateway Port
Config.Description: Internal gateway port. Change if 18789 is already in use (must match Control UI Port).

Config.Name: PATH
Config.Description: System PATH including ~/.local/bin (persistent local tools), Homebrew, Bun, Cargo. Do not modify unless you know what you're doing.

Config.Name: Web Search API Key
Config.Description: (Optional) Brave Search API key for web search. Free tier: 2000 queries/month at brave.com/search/api
